frameworks.name: computational modeling of behavior
frameworks.one_line: fitting algorithmic models to behavioral data to probe latent cognitive variables

core_question: How should researchers relate computational models to behavioral data so that fits yield meaningful insights about cognition?
key_assumptions: behavior can be characterized by tractable algorithmic models
model parameters map onto latent cognitive or neural variables
rigorous fitting and validation procedures generalize across techniques
key_findings: ten pragmatic rules cover model specification, fitting, and validation for behavioral data
rules are illustrated with the simplest accessible modeling techniques and online code
most rules also extend to more advanced modeling techniques
interpretations: following these guidelines helps researchers avoid common pitfalls in behavioral modeling
principled modeling can reveal algorithms, neural correlates, and effects of drugs, illness, and interventions
limitations_noted: tutorial focuses on the simplest techniques accessible to beginners
